title: Prediksi Athletic Bilbao Vs Barcelona di La Liga: Blaugrana Incar Kemenangan Ketiga Beruntun
content:
Bola.com, Jakarta - Barcelona akan melanjutkan perburuan gelar La Liga 2025/2026 dengan menghadapi Athletic Bilbao di San Mames, Sabtu malam waktu setempat. Laga ini menjadi kesempatan bagi Blaugrana untuk meraih kemenangan ketiga secara beruntun di kompetisi kasta tertinggi Spanyol.

Tim asuhan Hansi Flick saat ini memimpin klasemen La Liga dengan keunggulan empat poin atas Real Madrid yang berada di posisi kedua. Dengan musim yang mulai memasuki fase penentuan, Barcelona dituntut menjaga konsistensi agar tetap berada di jalur juara.

Sementara itu, Athletic Bilbao menghadapi situasi berbeda. Tim asuhan Ernesto Valverde baru saja tersingkir dari Copa del Rey setelah kalah 0-1 dari Real Sociedad pada leg kedua semifinal. Kekalahan tersebut membuat Los Leones tersingkir dengan agregat 0-2.

Tersingkirnya Athletic dari Copa del Rey membuat fokus mereka kini sepenuhnya tertuju pada La Liga. Namun, posisi mereka di klasemen belum terlalu menjanjikan untuk bersaing di papan atas.

title: Iran war latest: Trump says supreme leader may be dead - as he changes tone on help from allies
content:
By Cordelia Lynch, international correspondent

The road into Tyre is lined with the yellow and green flags of Hezbollah. Billboards are filled with the faces of fighters who lost their lives in the many battles with Israel over the years. We're in the evacuation zone, the area of south Lebanon that Israel has told everyone to leave. And it's not long before we see the mounting human cost of the latest conflict this community is engulfed in.

A group of mourners is gathered by the side of the road at a temporary cemetery. Huddled around makeshift memorials, some weep, some hug, others stare blankly ahead. They're here to bury four men that they say were medics and social workers. They were not, they say, fighters.

Ehsan Dbouk, a cleric for the group, says they've had to use this site because the men's hometowns are no longer safe.

"We can't bury our martyrs in their villages on the frontline. We are dealing with an enemy that doesn't distinguish between killing fighters and killing civilians," he says.

That enemy they claim represents an existential threat. Israel frames the Iran-backed group, proscribed as a terrorist organisation in the UK, in exactly the same way. Neither side is showing any sign of backing down.

The IDF has rapidly expanded the evacuation zone here. Until three days ago, it stretched from the border in the south with Israel to the Latani River. That has now been extended further north to the Zahrani River, about 25 miles from the border, raising fears of a ground invasion.

More than 800 people have been killed so far in the country and hundreds of thousands of people have been displaced. But Ehshan dismisses allegations that Hezbollah is dragging the rest of the country into a war it doesn't want and cannot win.

"The displaced are part of the resistance," he says.

"Hezbollah was born from their homes. They are the fathers and mothers of those fighting on the frontlines."

You can see how battle-hardened those who stayed behind are. The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) are fighting more than a force in Hezbollah - they're battling a mindset. And after months of Israeli strikes in the middle of a ceasefire, they believe they are fighting a just war now more than ever.

Nada Harb, a mother and Hezbollah supporter, tells me, "I won't leave, I didn't in the previous wars. I was born in war. But there was no resistance then like Hezbollah. The Israelis used to come at night, break down the door, they kidnapped my brother, my father, my sister, my uncle, and no one was allowed to say anything."

At her home, she takes us to her balcony to show us three buildings hit by airstrikes. She is exposed, vulnerable, but determined. The IDF insists they're targeting Hezbollah's infrastructure and leadership here.

But the civilian impact is already huge. The bridges, they say, that Hezbollah is using are also critical to civilians. And hundreds of thousands have been forced to flee this war already- many with no power, no shelter and no say in what happens next.

title: US, Israel attack Iran updates: Khamenei, top security officials killed
content:
Trita Parsi, executive vice president of the Quincy Institute for Responsible Statecraft, says Trump’s latest comments on the US-Israeli attacks on Iran show his “desperation”.

“It is very clear that Trump has tremendous difficulty finding a justification for this war of choice that he has embarked on that actually will work with his base… If this goes on for another week or two, this is going to become a political disaster, so now he is suddenly, desperately, using all kinds of justifications,” Parsi told Al Jazeera.

Earlier on Sunday, Trump told the Daily Mail newspaper the conflict with Iran could last for four weeks. Parsi said this is likely an attempt to send a message to Tehran, despite the lack of support for the fighting in the US.

“He [Trump] has already reached out to Tehran offering a ceasefire. The Iranians have rejected it, and I think he [Trump] has come to the conclusion that his eagerness to make a quick end to this war has signalled a weakness, and the Iranians are going to try and take advantage of that and will not agree to a ceasefire until they have inflicted significant pain on the United States,” said Parsi.
